canopy.title: Tree canopy height
canopy.source: Meta & WRI High-Resolution Canopy Height v2 (CHMv2)
canopy.note: Canopy height from a machine-learning model on ~1 m imagery. It reads height, so it separates trees (tall) from grass, crops and parks (low) - not by colour. Shown at about 5.7 m per pixel; zoom in to pick out individual trees.

landcover.title: Land cover
landcover.note: Independent 10 m land-cover map. Cross-check for the greenery picture.

heat.title: Land surface temperature
heat.source: Landsat Collection 2 Level-2 (ST_B10), USGS/NASA via MS Planetary Computer
heat.note: Ground (surface) temperature on one clear day, not air temperature and not a climate average.

priority.title: Where to plant
priority.source: Derived from CHM canopy + WorldCover land cover + Landsat heat
priority.note: Open, unbuilt land that is hot with little or no canopy - vacant plots, bare ground, scrub, park and canal edges. It deliberately skips rooftops and built-up areas (no planting space) and farmland and water. Brighter, more magenta = hotter, plant first. Guidance from data, not a site survey.
priority.method: land cover in {shrubland, grassland, bare} (built-up excluded) AND canopy under 2 m, shaded by surface temperature.
